sourceMovie.title: Tango & Cash
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Tango & Cash works as a action movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The action and thriller blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Substitute
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Tango & Cash clicked for you, The Substitute (1996) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and thriller, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Pulp Fiction
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Tango & Cash clicked for you, Pulp Fiction (1994) is a strong next stop: both trade in thriller and comedy, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Tango & Cash?
faq.answer: Try The Substitute, Pulp Fiction, Jackie Brown—each shares DNA with Tango & Cash while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Tango & Cash?
faq.answer: Tango & Cash (1989) is best described as Action, Thriller, Comedy, Drama, Crime. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Tango & Cash worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.5/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this action lane.
